entry.title: [BIP Proposal] Output Script Descriptor Annotations
entry.summary:
The discussion around BIP392 for Silent Payment Output Script Descriptors has highlighted a potential issue in the recovery of wallet funds due to the resource-intensive scanning requirements of the protocol. A key point is that some metadata, like the birthday block height, while not required to determine output scripts, may be necessary for efficient fund recovery. This presents a challenge as a wallet backup with just a valid output descriptor might not be sufficient for a complete fund recovery.
This problem is not exclusive to silent payments; any descriptor-based backup faces similar challenges. Specifically, descriptors outline what to derive but not the starting point or the extent of the scan. Restoring a wallet with only a descriptor involves guesswork regarding parameters such as gap limit or starting block height, leading to potential inefficiency or missed funds if the guesses are too conservative.
To address this, the BIP proposal suggests adding annotations - key/value pairs appended to a descriptor expression using URL-like query string delimiters. These annotations aim to include necessary metadata directly in the descriptor string, utilizing characters from the BIP380 checksum character set. An annotated descriptor follows a specific format including keys for starting block height, gap limit (for BIP32 HD wallets), and max label index (for silent payments wallets). The values for these annotations represent the minimum required to recover all funds at the time of descriptor exportation, acknowledging that these values may increase over the wallet's life but should not decrease.
Annotations are designed to be a compact method to convey operational metadata essential for fund recovery without altering the scripts produced by the descriptor. They are intended for use strictly within the scope of aiding fund recovery and do not replace general wallet backups which may contain additional data such as labels. The full details of the BIP can be accessed at [https://github.com/craigraw/bips/blob/descriptorannotations/bip-descriptorannotations.mediawiki](https://github.com/craigraw/bips/blob/descriptorannotations/bip-descriptorannotations.mediawiki).
